questions.question: The `apt-get remove` command removes a package but leaves configuration files on the system.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt-get remove command removes packages but leaves configuration files on the system.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: With `apt-get --only-upgrade`, `install` can install a brand new package that is not already present.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt-get --only-upgrade option does not install new packages and only upgrades packages that are already installed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt` command is documented as a high-level command-line interface intended for end users.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt command provides a high-level commandline interface for the package management system and is intended as an end user interface.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: DNF exit code `0` means an error occurred but DNF handled it.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. DNF returns 0 when the operation was successful.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get download` command downloads the requested binary package into the current directory.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt-get download command downloads the given binary package into the current directory.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: DNF uses exit code `100` for the `check-update` case.
questions.explanation: Correct. DNF uses exit code 100 for the check-update case.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The DNF `--best` option lets DNF silently settle for an older directly requested package when the latest version cannot be installed.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. dnf --best option forces DNF to consider only the latest packages for directly requested packages and fail if the latest version cannot be installed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt full-upgrade` command never removes installed packages.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt full-upgrade command may remove currently installed packages if that is needed to upgrade the system as a whole.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get install` command never retrieves dependency packages automatically.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt-get install command retrieves and installs packages required by the specified package or packages.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get --no-upgrade` option prevents already installed command-line packages from being upgraded.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt-get --no-upgrade option prevents command-line packages from being upgraded if they are already installed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: DNF is documented as a package manager for RPM-based Linux distributions.
questions.explanation: Correct. DNF is a package manager for RPM-based Linux distributions.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get autoclean` command removes only package files that can no longer be downloaded.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt-get autoclean command removes only retrieved package files that can no longer be downloaded.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt upgrade` command only refreshes package metadata and does not install package upgrades.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt upgrade command is used to install available upgrades of packages currently installed on the system.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The DNF `--cacheonly` option runs from cache without updating it, even if the cache is expired.
questions.explanation: Correct. dnf --cacheonly option runs entirely from the system cache and uses it even when it is expired.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The DNF `--allowerasing` option forbids erasing installed packages during dependency resolution.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. dnf --allowerasing option allows erasing installed packages to resolve dependencies.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt upgrade` command does not remove existing installed packages.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt upgrade command never removes existing installed packages.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt autoremove` command removes only packages that the user explicitly installed by hand.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt autoremove command removes packages that were automatically installed to satisfy dependencies and are no longer needed.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get` documentation says `update` should be performed before `upgrade` or `dist-upgrade`.
questions.explanation: Correct. An apt-get update should always be performed before apt-get upgrade or apt-get dist-upgrade.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get --download-only` option retrieves package files without unpacking or installing them.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt-get --download-only option retrieves package files but does not unpack or install them.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get purge` command keeps configuration files in place so the package can be restored quickly.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt-get purge command removes packages and deletes configuration files too.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get update` command installs every upgradable package on the system.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt-get update command resynchronizes the package index files from their sources.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get clean` command leaves all retrieved package files in the cache.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt-get clean command removes everything but the lock file from /var/cache/apt/archives/ and /var/cache/apt/archives/partial/.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Removing a package with `apt remove` can leave modified user configuration files behind.
questions.explanation: Correct. Removing a package with apt leaves usually small modified user configuration files behind.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt list` command can show only installed packages, not upgradeable or all available versions.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt list command can display installed, upgradeable, or all available versions of packages.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The DNF `--disablerepo` option permanently disables repositories until they are manually re-enabled later.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. dnf --disablerepo option temporarily disables active repositories for the current dnf command.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt edit-sources` command edits `sources.list` files and provides basic sanity checks.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt edit-sources command lets you edit sources.list files in your preferred text editor while providing basic sanity checks.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The DNF `--assumeno` option automatically answers no to all questions.
questions.explanation: Correct. dnf --assumeno option automatically answers no for all questions.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt search` command can search available packages for regex terms and display matches.
questions.explanation: Correct. apt search command can be used to search for given regex terms in the list of available packages and display matches.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: When used with `upgrade`, `apt-get --with-new-pkgs` allows package removals if dependency resolution needs them.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. When used with upgrade, the apt-get --with-new-pkgs option allows installing new dependencies but never removes packages.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `apt-get check` command automatically reinstalls packages to fix dependency problems.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. apt-get check command is a diagnostic tool that updates the package cache and checks for broken dependencies.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.
